title: C Major Scale
description:
The C Major scale is the most fundamental scale in Western music. 
It contains no sharps or flats, making it unique and easy to learn.

Notes:
C έΗΤ D έΗΤ E έΗΤ F έΗΤ G έΗΤ A έΗΤ B έΗΤ C

Key Characteristics:
έΑλ All white keys
έΑλ Clear, bright sound
έΑλ Foundation for music theory

title: C Natural Minor Scale
description:
The C Natural Minor scale introduces a more emotional and expressive sound compared to its major counterpart.

C έΗΤ D έΗΤ Eέβφ έΗΤ F έΗΤ G έΗΤ Aέβφ έΗΤ Bέβφ έΗΤ C

Key Characteristics:
έΑλ Uses three flats: Eέβφ, Aέβφ, and Bέβφ

title: G Major Scale
description:
The G Major scale introduces your first sharp! 
It is widely used in folk and pop music.

title: D Major Scale
description:
The D Major scale is a favorite for string players and pianist alike.

D έΗΤ E έΗΤ Fέβψ έΗΤ G έΗΤ A έΗΤ B έΗΤ Cέβψ έΗΤ D

title: A Natural Minor Scale
description:
The A Natural Minor scale is the relative minor of C Major and is often the first minor scale beginners learn.

Notes:
A έΗΤ B έΗΤ C έΗΤ D έΗΤ E έΗΤ F έΗΤ G έΗΤ A

Key Characteristics:
έΑλ No sharps or flats
έΑλ Melancholic and expressive sound
έΑλ Commonly used in classical, pop, and cinematic music
